body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

The scenario's User-observable signal is the ABSENCE of a `→ working` transition: the user invokes a slash command that responds locally with no model round-trip, and the session stays `ready` throughout. Pi supports this natively — its interactive REPL exposes a set of in-process slash commands (a 30-entry slash menu). `/session` ('Show session info and stats') was verified live against pi 0.70.0: issued after a warm-up turn, it renders session stats (ID, message counts, tokens, cost) directly in the TUI and appends ZERO lines to the rollout transcript (line count unchanged before/after — 5 lines both times). It executes synchronously in the REPL without an LLM round-trip.

irrlicht observes the scenario by what it does NOT see: pi's transcript is FilesUnderRoot (append-only JSONL under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/), and the pi parser only promotes ready→working on `message` events (assistant/user/toolResult roles) or a top-level `compaction` event. A local slash command writes none of these, so no `transcript_activity` is recorded and no `state_transition` to working fires. The recording captures exactly one ready→working→ready arc (the warm-up turn) followed by a continuous ready dwell across the slash window, then a single process_exited at REPL teardown.

Confidence 0.7 (not higher) because the observable is purely negative — proving a slash command was issued at all is not visible in events.jsonl; the spec asserts the absence of a second working transition during the post-slash dwell window, which is the strongest available observable for a no-LLM local command on a non-lazy transcript adapter.

WHY a warm-up turn FIRST: pi creates its rollout transcript only after the first user message, so the driver's resolve_transcript needs a real turn to bind the UUID; the warm-up also supplies the one legitimate working→ready arc to contrast against the slash command's silence.
caveats: The observable is the ABSENCE of a working transition — a local slash command leaves no transcript trace, so 'a slash was issued' is itself invisible to events.jsonl; the spec asserts the negative (no → working, no waiting) across the post-slash ready dwell.

/session is the verified no-LLM local command (pi 0.70.0 slash menu: 'Show session info and stats'); /usage, /status, /help, /cost are NOT pi commands (the slash menu shows no match). Avoid /clear and /new (session-reset) and /compact (real model call).

The live REPL may briefly emit a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready before the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears; the spec anchors session_birth to the pre-session-tolerant 'ready within 2s' match and pins the turn arc + slash dwell to the rollout via same_session_as (turn_start anchored to recording start with no pin binds the FIRST working — necessarily the rollout).

sources.note: Identity.Name=pi, Source variant FilesUnderRoot under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, ExactName{Name: "pi"} process matcher.
Only message events (assistant/user/toolResult) or a top-level compaction event promote ready→working; a local slash command writes none, so no state machinery fires.
/session renders session stats in the TUI and appends ZERO lines to the rollout (transcript line count unchanged before/after). /usage,/status,/help,/cost produce no slash-menu match.
Interactive tmux REPL driver supporting send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep; the slash step types the /session command + Enter inside the live REPL.
